18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
19	// Durable knobs (Assignee, ReviewMaxRetries, NoProgressCooldown,
20	// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose, HeartbeatInterval, Harness, Model, Provider,
21	// ModelRef, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are stripped — they live on
22	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's rcfg argument.
23	// Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation runtime intent remain.
24	//
25	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Bead 6.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log          io.Writer
28		EventSink    io.Writer
29		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
30		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
31		Once         bool
32		PollInterval time.Duration
33		NoReview     bool
34		LabelFilter  string
35		SessionID    string
36		WorkerID     string
37		ProjectRoot  string
38	}
39	
40	// RunWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to Run. It accepts a sealed
41	// ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and a ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime (plumbing
42	// + per-invocation intent) and delegates to Run under the hood. Behavior
43	// is identical to Run with an equivalently-populated ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
44	//
45	// Stage 1 of SD-024: this method exists alongside Run; production callers
46	// have not migrated yet.
47	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) RunWithConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
48		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
49			Assignee:                rcfg.Assignee(),
50			Once:                    runtime.Once,
51			PollInterval:            runtime.PollInterval,
52			NoProgressCooldown:      rcfg.NoProgressCooldown(),
53			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose(),
54			HeartbeatInterval:       rcfg.HeartbeatInterval(),
55			Log:                     runtime.Log,
56			NoReview:                runtime.NoReview,
57			ReviewMaxRetries:        rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries(),
58			EventSink:               runtime.EventSink,
59			ProgressCh:              runtime.ProgressCh,
60			PreClaimHook:            runtime.PreClaimHook,
61			WorkerID:                runtime.WorkerID,
62			ProjectRoot:             runtime.ProjectRoot,
63			Harness:                 rcfg.Harness(),
64			Model:                   rcfg.Model(),
65			Profile:                 rcfg.Profile(),
66			Provider:                rcfg.Provider(),
67			ModelRef:                rcfg.ModelRef(),
68			SessionID:               runtime.SessionID,
69			LabelFilter:             runtime.LabelFilter,
70			MinTier:                 rcfg.MinTier(),
71			MaxTier:                 rcfg.MaxTier(),
72		}
73		return w.Run(ctx, opts)
74	}

76	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
77	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
78	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
79	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

82		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
83		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
84		WorkerID    string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
85		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
86		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`

88		Status      string `json:"status"`

91		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
92		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
93		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
94		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
95		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
96		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
97		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
98		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
99		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
100		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for

102		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
103		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).

114		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile

133	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
134	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
135	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
136	// the current attempt.
137	//
138	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
139	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
140	// leaves the bead open.
141	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
142		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
143	}
144	
145	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
146	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
147	
148	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
149		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
150	}

185	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
186	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
187	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
188	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
189	// these directly from the channel.
190	//
191	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
192	type ProgressEvent struct {
193		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
194		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`
195		WorkerID  string    `json:"worker_id"`
196		ProjectID string    `json:"project_id"`
197		BeadID    string    `json:"bead_id"`
198		Harness   string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
199		Model     string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
200		Profile   string    `json:"profile,omitempty"`
201		Phase     string    `json:"phase"`
202		PhaseSeq  int       `json:"phase_seq"`
203		Heartbeat bool      `json:"heartbeat"`
204		TS        time.Time `json:"ts"`
205		ElapsedMS int64     `json:"elapsed_ms"`
206		Message   string    `json:"message,omitempty"`
207	}

209	type ExecuteBeadLoopOptions struct {
210		Assignee                string
211		Once                    bool
212		PollInterval            time.Duration
213		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
214		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
215		// HeartbeatInterval, if > 0, overrides bead.HeartbeatInterval for this
216		// worker's claim heartbeat loop. Tests use this to shorten the tick.
217		HeartbeatInterval time.Duration
218		Log               io.Writer
219		// NoReview, when true, skips the post-merge review step even when
220		// ExecuteBeadWorker.Reviewer is configured. Use for doc-only beads or
221		// tight iteration loops where review latency is not acceptable.
222		NoReview bool
223	
224		// ReviewMaxRetries caps the number of reviewer attempts per committed
225		// result_rev before the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required`
226		// event and parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or negative falls back
227		// to DefaultReviewMaxRetries (3). The counter is scoped to a single
228		// result_rev: a fresh result_rev resets the counter, and iterations
229		// without a committed result_rev (--no-merge, execution-failed) do not
230		// consume the budget because the reviewer is not invoked.
231		ReviewMaxRetries int
232	
233		// EventSink receives structured JSONL progress events emitted at
234		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end milestones.
235		// When nil, no structured events are written. Log (terminal text)
236		// is independent and still emitted for human operators.
237		EventSink io.Writer
238	
239		// ProgressCh, when non-nil, receives FEAT-006 ProgressEvents for each
240		// bead execution managed by this loop. The caller is responsible for
241		// draining the channel; the loop sends non-blocking (events are dropped
242		// if the channel is full). The loop does NOT close this channel; the
243		// caller (WorkerManager.runWorker) closes it after Run returns.
244		ProgressCh chan<- ProgressEvent
245	
246		// PreClaimHook, when non-nil, is called before Store.Claim for each
247		// candidate bead. If it returns an error the bead is not claimed and the
248		// loop continues to the next iteration (ctx is NOT cancelled). A nil hook
249		// disables the check. Server and CLI paths wire a real implementation
250		// backed by LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck; tests may inject nil
251		// or a stub that always returns nil.
252		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
253	
254		// Worker/session metadata included in loop.start events so log
255		// aggregators can correlate structured output with the executing
256		// harness/worker. None of these are required.
257		WorkerID    string
258		ProjectRoot string
259		Harness     string
260		Model       string
261		Profile     string
262		Provider    string
263		ModelRef    string
264		SessionID   string
265		LabelFilter string
266	
267		// MinTier and MaxTier bound the tier escalation range when the executor
268		// uses tier-based auto-escalation. Empty string uses the defaults (cheap
269		// and smart). Ignored when the executor has escalation disabled (e.g. an
270		// explicit --harness or --model override was specified).
271		MinTier string
272		MaxTier string
273	}

286	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
287	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
288	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
289	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
290		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
291		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
292		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
293		Now                 func() time.Time
294		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
295		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
296		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
297		Reviewer BeadReviewer
298	}
299	
300	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to opts.ProgressCh non-blocking.
301	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
302	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
303		if ch == nil {
304			return
305		}
306		select {
307		case ch <- evt:
308		default:
309		}
310	}
311	
312	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
313	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) ProgressEvent {
314		return ProgressEvent{
315			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
316			AttemptID: attemptID,
317			WorkerID:  workerID,
318			ProjectID: projectID,
319			BeadID:    beadID,
320			Harness:   opts.Harness,
321			Model:     opts.Model,
322			Profile:   opts.Profile,
323			Phase:     phase,
324			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
325			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
326			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
327			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
328		}
329	}
330	
331	func randomProgressID() string {
332		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
333	}
334	
335	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
336		if w.Store == nil {
337			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
338		}
339		if w.Executor == nil {
340			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
341		}
342	
343		now := w.Now
344		if now == nil {
345			now = time.Now
346		}
347		assignee := opts.Assignee
348		if assignee == "" {
349			assignee = "ddx"
350		}
351		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
352		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
353			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
354		}
355		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
356		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
357			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
358		}
359		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
360		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
361			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
362		}
363	
364		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
365		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
366	
367		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
368			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
369		}
370	
371		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
372			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
373			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
374			"harness":      opts.Harness,
375			"model":        opts.Model,
376			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
377			"assignee":     assignee,
378			"once":         opts.Once,
379		})
380		defer func() {
381			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
382				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
383				"successes":           result.Successes,
384				"failures":            result.Failures,
385				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
386			})
387		}()
388	
389		for {
390			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
391			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
392			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
393			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
394			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
395				return result, err
396			}
397	
398			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
399			if err != nil {
400				return result, err
401			}
402			if !ok {
403				if result.Attempts == 0 {
404					result.NoReadyWork = true
405					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
406						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
407							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
408								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
409								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
410								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
411								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
412								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
413							}
414						}
415					}
416				}
417				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
418					return result, nil
419				}
420				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
421					return result, err
422				}
423				continue
424			}
425	
426			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
427	
428			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
429			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
430			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
431			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
432				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
433					if opts.Log != nil {
434						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
435					}
436					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
437						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
438						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
439					})
440					continue
441				}
442			}
443	
444			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
445				continue
446			}
447	
448			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
449				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
450				"title":    candidate.Title,
451				"assignee": assignee,
452			})
453	
454			if opts.Log != nil {
455				if candidate.Title != "" {
456					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
457				} else {
458					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
459				}
460			}
461	
462			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
463			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
464			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
465			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
466			runStart := now()
467			phaseSeq := 0
468			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
469				phaseSeq++
470				emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
471					opts.WorkerID, opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
472					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(), opts,
473				))
474			}
475	
476			nextPhase("queueing", false)
477	
478			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
479			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
480			hbWG.Add(1)
481			go func(beadID string) {
482				defer hbWG.Done()
483				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
484				defer ticker.Stop()
485				for {
486					select {
487					case <-hbCtx.Done():
488						return
489					case <-ticker.C:
490						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
491					}
492				}
493			}(candidate.ID)
494	
495			nextPhase("running", false)
496	
497			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
498			hbCancel()
499			hbWG.Wait()
500			if err != nil {
501				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
502					BeadID: candidate.ID,
503					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
504					Detail: err.Error(),
505				}
506			}
507			if report.BeadID == "" {
508				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
509			}
510			if report.Status == "" {
511				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
512			}
513			if report.Detail == "" {
514				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
515			}
516	
517			result.Attempts++
518	
519			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
520				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
521				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
522				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
523				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
524				reviewApproved := true
525				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || opts.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
526	
527				if reviewSkipped {
528					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
529						return result, err
530					}
531				}
532	
533				if w.Reviewer != nil && !opts.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
534					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
535					if reviewErr != nil {
536						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
537						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
538						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
539						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
540						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
541						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
542						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
543						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
544						attemptCount := prior + 1
545						maxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
546						if maxRetries <= 0 {
547							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
548						}
549						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
550							InputBytes:  0,
551							OutputBytes: 0,
552						}
553						if reviewRes != nil {
554							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
555								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
556								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
557								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
558								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
559								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
560							}
561						}
562						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
563							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
564							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
565								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
566								Summary:   class,
567								Body:      body,
568								Actor:     assignee,
569								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
570								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
571							})
572							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
573							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
574							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
575							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
576							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
577							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
578						} else {
579							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
580							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
581								Kind:      "review-error",
582								Summary:   class,
583								Body:      body,
584								Actor:     assignee,
585								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
586								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
587							})
588						}
589					} else {
590						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
591						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
592						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
593						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
594						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
595						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
596						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
597						// log if the artifact write failed.
598						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(opts.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
599						if artifactErr != nil && opts.Log != nil {
600							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
601						}
602	
603						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
604							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
605							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
606							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
607							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
608							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
609						}
610						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
611						case VerdictApprove:
612							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
613							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
614							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
615							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
616								Kind:      "review",
617								Summary:   "APPROVE",
618								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
619								Actor:     assignee,
620								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
621								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
622							})
623							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
624								return result, cerr
625							}
626						case VerdictRequestChanges:
627							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
628							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
629							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
630							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
631							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
632								Kind:      "review",
633								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
634								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
635								Actor:     assignee,
636								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
637								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
638							})
639							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
640							if reopenNotes == "" {
641								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
642							}
643							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
644								return result, reopenErr
645							}
646							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
647							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
648							reviewApproved = false
649						case VerdictBlock:
650							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
651							if rationale == "" {
652								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
653									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
654									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
655									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
656									Actor:     assignee,
657									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
658									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
659								})
660								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
661								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
662								report.ReviewRationale = ""
663								reviewApproved = false
664								break
665							}
666							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
667							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
668							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
669								Kind:      "review",
670								Summary:   "BLOCK",
671								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
672								Actor:     assignee,
673								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
674								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
675							})
676							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
677							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
678								return result, reopenErr
679							}
680							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
681							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
682							reviewApproved = false
683						}
684					}
685				}
686	
687				if reviewApproved {
688					result.Successes++
689					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
690				} else {
691					result.Failures++
692					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
693				}
694			} else {
695				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
696					return result, err
697				}
698				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
699					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
700					if cerr != nil {
701						return result, cerr
702					}
703					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale)
704					if aerr != nil {
705						return result, aerr
706					}
707					if satisfied {
708						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
709						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
710						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
711						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
712						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
713						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
714						// satisfied beads).
715						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
716						if evidence != "" {
717							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
718							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
719							report.Detail = evidence
720						}
721						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
722						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
723							return result, cerr
724						}
725						result.Successes++
726						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
727					} else {
728						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
729						// move on to other beads.
730						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
731							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
732							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
733								return result, cerr
734							}
735							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
736						}
737						result.Failures++
738						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
739					}
740				} else {
741					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
742						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
743						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
744							return result, err
745						}
746						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
747					}
748					result.Failures++
749					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
750				}
751			}
752	
753			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
754	
755			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
756			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
757			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
758			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
759			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
760				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
761					return result, err
762				}
763			}
764	
765			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
766			terminalPhase := "failed"
767			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
768				terminalPhase = "done"
769			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
770				terminalPhase = "preserved"
771			}
772			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
773			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
774			if finalAttemptID == "" {
775				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
776			}
777			phaseSeq++
778			emitProgress(opts.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
779				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
780				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
781				WorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,
782				ProjectID: opts.ProjectRoot,
783				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
784				Harness:   opts.Harness,
785				Model:     opts.Model,
786				Profile:   opts.Profile,
787				Phase:     terminalPhase,
788				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
789				Heartbeat: false,
790				TS:        now().UTC(),
791				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
792				Message:   report.Detail,
793			})
794	
795			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
796				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
797				"status":               report.Status,
798				"detail":               report.Detail,
799				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
800				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
801				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
802				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
803				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
804				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
805			})
806	
807			if opts.Log != nil {
808				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
809			}
810	
811			if opts.Once {
812				return result, nil
813			}
814		}
815	}

817	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
818		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
819		if err != nil {
820			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
821		}
822		for _, candidate := range ready {
823			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
824				continue
825			}
826			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
827				continue
828			}
829			return candidate, true, nil
830		}
831		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
832	}

916	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
917	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
918	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
919	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
920	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
921	// never break the core execute-loop.
922	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
923		if sink == nil {
924			return
925		}
926		if data == nil {
927			data = map[string]any{}
928		}
929		entry := map[string]any{
930			"session_id": sessionID,
931			"type":       eventType,
932			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
933			"data":       data,
934		}
935		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
936		if err != nil {
937			return
938		}
939		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
940		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
941	}

943	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
944	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
945	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
946		switch report.Status {
947		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
948			shortRev := report.ResultRev
949			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
950				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
951			}
952			if shortRev != "" {
953				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
954			}
955			return "merged"
956		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
957			return "already_satisfied"
958		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
959			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
960				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
961			}
962			return "no_changes"
963		default:
964			detail := report.Detail
965			if detail == "" {
966				detail = report.Status
967			}
968			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
969				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
970			}
971			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
972		}
973	}

975	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
976	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
977	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
978	
979	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
980	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
981	
982	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
983	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
984	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
985	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
986	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
987		if rationale == "" {
988			return false
989		}
990		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
991	}

993	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
994	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
995	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
996	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
997	//
998	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
999	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
1000	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
1001	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
1002	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale string) (bool, string, error) {
1003		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
1004			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
1005		}
1006		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
1007			evidence := rationale
1008			return true, evidence, nil
1009		}
1010		if noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose {
1011			return true, fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount), nil
1012		}
1013		return false, "", nil
1014	}

1016	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
1017		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
1018			return false
1019		}
1020		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev

1023	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1024	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1025	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1026	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1027	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1028	//     embed the class in their message).
1029	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1030	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1031	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1032	//     matches.
1033	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1034		if reviewRes != nil {
1035			switch reviewRes.Error {
1036			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1037				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1038				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1039				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1040				return reviewRes.Error
1041			}
1042		}
1043		msg := ""
1044		if reviewErr != nil {
1045			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1046		}
1047		switch {
1048		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1049			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1050		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1051			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1052		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1053			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1054		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1055			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1056		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1057			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1058		}
1059		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1060	}

1062	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1063	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1064	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1065	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1066	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1067	
1068	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1069	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1070	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1071	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1072	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1073		if resultRev == "" {
1074			return 0
1075		}
1076		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1077		if err != nil {
1078			return 0
1079		}
1080		n := 0
1081		for _, ev := range events {
1082			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1083				continue
1084			}
1085			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1086			if m == nil {
1087				continue
1088			}
1089			if m[1] == resultRev {
1090				n++
1091			}
1092		}
1093		return n
1094	}
1095	
1096	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1097	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1098	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1099	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1100	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1101	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1102		var b strings.Builder
1103		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1104		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1105		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1106		if message != "" {
1107			b.WriteString("\n")
1108			b.WriteString(message)
1109		}
1110		return b.String()
1111	}

1113	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1114		if d <= 0 {
1115			return nil
1116		}
1117		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1118		defer timer.Stop()
1119		select {
1120		case <-ctx.Done():
1121			return ctx.Err()
1122		case <-timer.C:
1123			return nil
1124		}
1125	}

165	// TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead verifies that when PreClaimHook returns an
166	// error the bead is not claimed and the loop moves on (NoReadyWork path).
167	func TestPreClaimHookDivergedSkipsBead(t *testing.T) {
168		store, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
169	
170		executed := []string{}
171		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
172			Store: store,
173			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
174				executed = append(executed, beadID)
175				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "abc"}, nil
176			}),
177		}
178	
179		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
180			Assignee: "worker",
181			Once:     true,
182			PreClaimHook: func(ctx context.Context) error {
183				return fmt.Errorf("diverged: local=aaa origin=bbb")
184			},
185		})
186		require.NoError(t, err)
187		require.NotNil(t, result)
188	
189		// Executor must not have been called.
190		assert.Empty(t, executed, "executor should not run when pre-claim hook returns error")
191		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
192	
193		// Bead must still be open (not claimed).
194		got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
195		require.NoError(t, err)
196		assert.NotEqual(t, "claimed", got.Status, "bead should not have been claimed")
197	}
